title: Causal latent tyre-state filter with uncertainty features
summary: Recent F1 tyre-degradation work models wear as a latent state with pit-stop resets, and motorsport strategy literature treats degradation as the main driver of stop timing.
rationale: Recent F1 tyre-degradation work models wear as a latent state with pit-stop resets, and motorsport strategy literature treats degradation as the main driver of stop timing. That maps well to this tabular setting: the current code uses observed degradation and EWMs, but not an explicit hidden wear state or state uncertainty. A causal filter can summarize whether a driver is deteriorating faster than raw TyreLife suggests.
implementation_hint: Within each (Year,Race,Driver,Stint), build a one-step causal filter from LapTime_Delta, Cumulative_Degradation, TyreLife, Compound, and LapNumber. Emit filtered level, filtered slope, innovation, and posterior uncertainty; reset at PitStop=1. Feed these as additional numeric features into the existing tree stack, computed strictly from past laps only.
expected_effect: Medium upside if TyreLife and degradation are noisy proxies for true wear; should be most useful on dry compounds and long green-flag stints.
risk: Easy to leak if the filter uses contemporaneous or future race-level aggregates; over-parameterized filters may just recreate current EWMs with more variance.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
